title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod researchers started a GoFundMe to protect the endangered North Atlantic right whale, aiming to raise $15,000.
The Marineland Right Whale Project funds research, including drone surveys and high-resolution photography, to aid whale conservation.
With only a few hundred whales left, major threats include vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements, recent deaths highlight these dangers.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a season after being acquired from the Raiders by the Patriots.
Wes Welker was traded from the Dolphins in 2007, becoming a prolific slot receiver alongside Randy Moss.
Josh Gordon showed potential in 2018 but off-field issues limited his impact despite winning a ring with the Patriots.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected for arguing a check swing call in the eighth inning of the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Trevor Story was upset after umpire C.B. Bucknor's strike three call; Cora intervened to prevent Story's ejection.
Red Sox exhausted challenges early; Roman Anthony was critical of himself for losing the team's final challenge.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call, claiming he didn't swing and the call wasn't confirmed.
Alex Cora was ejected after arguing with Bucknor over the check swing call during the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Despite Trevor Story's home run and Wilyer Abreu's tying homer, Red Sox lost to open the season 1-1.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha missed the Milano-Cortina Olympics due to a concussion but has scored 11 goals since returning.
Zacha has become a top goal scorer for the Bruins, netting 11 goals and 19 points in 16 post-Olympic games.
Since arriving from New Jersey, Zacha set a career-high with 26 goals this season, contributing to Boston's playoff push.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray's Red Sox debut ended in a no-decision after allowing four runs in four innings against the Cincinnati Reds.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora noted Gray's improvement after a challenging start, despite limited innings in the 6-5 loss.
Boston's bullpen performed well after Gray's exit, but Reds won with a walk-off hit in the 11th inning.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands attended the 'No Kings' rally at Boston Common, featuring speeches from state leaders and performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
The Boston event was part of over 3,300 nationwide protests, with more than 160 occurring in Massachusetts, according to organizers.
Speakers like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren addressed political issues, urging action against perceived democratic threats.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 17-song set at the House of Blues, using theatrical shock rock and fake blood sprays.
Lead singer Michael Bishop led the band in gothic costumes, incorporating social and political satire.
The setlist included "F–k This Place," "Tormentor," and an encore featuring "Pink Pony Club" and "Sick of You."

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum listed as questionable for Sunday's game against Charlotte Hornets due to injury concerns.
Tatum played 36 minutes against Atlanta, despite recent return, and expressed frustration with his shooting performance.
Nikola Vucevic is out with a right ring finger fracture; Boston won 8 of the last 10 games with Tatum playing.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest with speeches from prominent politicians and musical performances.
Veterans and church groups attended the protest due to concerns about democracy and the country's direction.
Protesters, some in costume, expressed desires to uphold democratic values, drawing inspiration from historical rejection of monarchy.
